visual_attributes.morphology: large parrot with a robust body and long, narrow, curved beak
distinctive_features: large, narrow, curved grey-brown upper beak
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: 46 to 50 cm (18 to 20 in) in length, weighing 750 to 1,000 g (1.65 to 2.20 lb)
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: broad wings, short tail with black tip, feather shafts projecting at tail tip

domain_specific_visual.beak_length: male's upper beak is 12–14% longer than female's
domain_specific_visual.juvenile_features: yellow eye-rings and cere, orange-yellow lower beak

functional_attributes.primary_function: omnivorous feeding and ecological role in alpine regions

domain_specific_functional.nesting_behavior: nests in burrows or crevices among tree roots
domain_specific_functional.cognitive_abilities: tool use and problem-solving similar to primates

relationships.description: The kea is native to the forested and alpine regions of the South Island.
The kea is closely related to the New Zealand kākā within the genus Nestor.
The kea is part of the parrot superfamily Strigopoidea, related to the flightless kākāpō.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: forested and alpine regions of the South Island
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: known for intelligence and curiosity, protected under Wildlife Act
